title: Graphs
analogy: A graph is like a social network — each person is a node and friendships are edges. Some friendships are one-way (directed), some are mutual (undirected).

overview.summary: Graphs are the most general data structure — they model relationships between pairs of objects using nodes (vertices) and edges.
learningObjectives: Understand directed vs undirected, weighted vs unweighted graphs.
Represent graphs using adjacency lists and adjacency matrices.
overview.interviewRelevance: Graph problems appear in 20%+ of interviews — networks, dependencies, shortest paths, and topological ordering.

paragraphs: Google Maps models roads as a graph: intersections are nodes, roads are edges, and distances are weights.
Social networks, flight routes, package dependency trees, and neural networks are all graphs.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Any problem involving connections between entities — where you need to traverse, search, or find paths — is a graph problem in disguise.

definition.technicalDefinition: A graph G = (V, E) consists of a set of vertices V and a set of edges E, where each edge connects two vertices. Edges can be directed or undirected, and optionally carry weights.

vocabulary.definition: A fundamental unit in the graph.
A mapping from each vertex to its list of neighbors.
Number of edges connected to a vertex.

keyProperties: Adjacency list: O(V + E) space. Adjacency matrix: O(V²) space.
Traversal (BFS/DFS) visits all vertices and edges: O(V + E).
Directed graphs can have cycles; DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) cannot.

operations.name: Build Adjacency List
operations.description: Convert edge pairs into a dictionary mapping each node to its neighbors.

code.python:
from collections import defaultdict

def build_graph(edges):
    graph = defaultdict(list)
    for u, v in edges:
        graph[u].append(v)
        graph[v].append(u)  # undirected
    return graph

operations.visualNote: Nodes arranged in a circle with edges drawn between connected pairs.

practice.title: Number of Islands
practice.keyInsight: Grid as implicit graph, count connected components.

practice.title: Course Schedule
practice.keyInsight: Directed graph cycle detection via topological sort.

practice.title: Graph Valid Tree
practice.keyInsight: A tree is a connected acyclic graph with V-1 edges.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the space complexity of an adjacency list representation?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Each vertex has a list entry, and each edge is stored twice (once per endpoint).

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Given N cities and M roads between them, determine if you can reach every city from city 0.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Connectivity in a network is a fundamental graph problem solved via BFS/DFS.
